data.body: PASS-1 / E4 (assumption verification): The plan's load-bearing premise — that the gate "never specifies how to disable/roll back the gate itself" and lacks a "system-level kill switch" — is contradicted by the shipped code. `src/rebar/config.py:260` declares `require_plan_review_for_claim: bool = False` (DEFAULT OFF, fail-open), and the inline comment at config.py:258-259 states "Default off => `claim` keeps today's behavior; turning it off is the rollback (an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed)." The claim-path check `_plan_review_precheck` (src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:661-662) returns `None` (allow, claim unchanged) when the flag is off, and additionally fails OPEN on unreadable config (transition.py:652-660). rebar.toml:20 likewise documents "Turning this off is the rollback." So AC#1 (a config flag disables the gate => claim unchanged, fail-open) and the kill-switch premise of the Why section appear to ALREADY be satisfied by the existing implementation. The author should re-ground the plan against this code: the genuine remaining gap is narrower (the persisted SIGNATURE/REVIEW_RESULT event artifacts + deploy-ordering/old+new coexistence for the sidecar event type — see final_gate_cycle3 T12 note that "rollback reverts gate behavior but not the persisted event-log artifacts"), not the absence of an off-switch.
